assessment_items.question_content:
**Charlemagne instructed his artists at court to revive the artistic styles that were present in the days of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/881a179e902a7c404274259c1977f5dc.png)
hints:
**Charlemagne instructed his artists at court to revive the artistic styles that were present in the days of Constantine. This period is called the Carolingian Renaissance.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Lindau Gospels Cover shows the Crucifixion in the [[☃ dropdown 1]] Christ type.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f82c73f137c98451234cd4a0319e817.png)
hints:
**The Lindau Gospels Cover shows the Crucifixion in the Triumphant Christ type. Christ is upright and alert on the cross and does not appear to be suffering.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The influence of the classical tradition is present in the . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22be88c475540035842ca0302cef026e.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The influence of the classical tradition is present in the cloth and drapery that Christ and the other figures wear.** 

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

choices.content: cloth and drapery that the figures wear.
subject of the Crucifixion for the cover of this book.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The figures are examples of [[☃ dropdown 1]], which is a metalworking technique where the artist hammers out the image on the reverse side.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22be88c475540035842ca0302cef026e.png)
hints:
**The figures are examples of repoussé, which is a metalworking technique where the artist hammers out the image on the reverse side.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The iconography of the Lindau Gospels cover foretells the contents of the book within.   Match the iconographical detail to its symbolic meaning.** 



[[☃ matcher 1]]

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e4f89c23569ac945cf889e667fe167a.jpg)
hints: **In contrast to later representations that emphasize Christ's suffering, the Triumphant Christ shown here symbolizes his Divine nature.**
**Jewels are used to recall biblical descriptions to the Heavenly Jerusalem in the Book of Revelations. **

**In addition to representing the cross of Christ's crucifixion, the raised cruciform shape recalls the basilica-plan used in Christian churches. ** 

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

right: The figure of the Triumphant Christ
The raised cruciform shape surrounding Christ
The jewels and precious stones  set in gold

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement explains why Carolingian rulers wanted to revive Classical traditions? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The Roman emperor Constantine served as a bridge between pagan rule and the rise of the Christian era.   **

**The Carolingian revival of Classical traditions reflected a desire to reform and standardize the Church, and the tendency to humanize Christian ideals.  They looked to early Christian texts and drawings as sources revealing the strong influence of pagan style.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/carolingian/v/lindau-gospels-cover)**

choices.content: Charlemagne's empire served as a bridge between pagan rule and the rise of the Christian era
The rediscovery of lost Christian documents and drawings that emphasized Classical traditions
The desire to reform and standardize the Church and society
The tendency to humanize spiritual ideals and beliefs
